back_translation: "At that time, the kingdom of heaven will be like ten virgin women who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
Of those women, five were foolish ones, and five were wise ones.
For when the foolish ones took their lamps with them, they did not bring oil along with them.
But the wise ones put oil in containers and took it along together with their lamps.
When the bridegroom was delayed, all the women became drowsy and fell asleep.
But at midnight, a cry rang out: 'Behold, the bridegroom has come! Go out to meet him!'
Then all those virgin women woke up and trimmed their lamps to be ready.
The foolish ones said to the wise ones, 'Please share some of your oil with us, for our lamps are about to go out.'
But the wise ones answered, 'The oil probably will not be enough for us and for you. You had better go to the sellers and buy oil for yourselves.'
While they were going to buy oil, the bridegroom arrived. The women who were prepared went into the wedding feast with him, and the door was shut.
Later, the other virgin women also arrived and said, 'Master sir, master sir, please open the door for us.'
But he answered, 'I tell you the truth, I do not know you.'
Therefore, keep watch, for you do not know the day or the hour."
For [the kingdom of heaven] is also like a man who was about to travel to a foreign land; he called his servants and entrusted his possessions to them.
He gave to one five talents, to another two talents, and to another one talent, each according to his ability. Then he went on his journey.
The one who had received the five talents immediately went and traded with that money, and he gained another five talents.
Likewise, the one who had received the two talents also gained another two talents.
But the one who had received one talent went out, dug a hole in the ground, and hid his master's money.
After a long time had passed, the master of those servants came back and settled accounts with them.
The one who had received five talents came in and brought another five talents to present, saying, 'Master sir, you entrusted five talents to me. Behold, I have gained another five talents.'
His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You were faithful in a small thing; I will put you in charge of more things. Come and share in the joy of your master.'
The one who had received two talents also came in and said, 'Master sir, you entrusted two talents to me. Behold, I have gained another two talents.'
His master said to him, 'Well done, good and faithful servant! You were faithful in a small thing; I will put you in charge of more things. Come and share in the joy of your master.'

As for the one who had received one talent, he came in and said, 'Master sir, I know that you are a hard-hearted man, reaping grain in places where you did not sow, and gathering in places where you did not scatter.
So I was afraid; I went out and hid your talent in the ground. Behold, here is what is yours.'
But his master answered, 'You wicked and lazy servant! Do you claim you knew that I reap grain where I did not sow, and gather where I did not scatter?
Therefore, you ought to have taken our money and deposited it with the bankers. When we returned, we would have received our money back together with interest.
Therefore, take the talent from him, and give it to the one who has ten talents.
For everyone who has will receive more and will have in overflowing abundance. But the one who does not have, even what he has will be confiscated from him.
As for the worthless servant, throw him out into the darkness outside, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'
When the Son of Man comes in his glory, and all the angels are with him, then he will be seated on his throne of glory.
All the nations will be gathered before his presence, and he will separate them from one another, just as a shepherd separates the flock of sheep from the flock of goats.
He will place the sheep on his right side, and the goats on the left side.

Then the Great King will speak to those at his right side, saying, 'Come, all you who have received blessing from my Father, receive as your inheritance the kingdom that was prepared for you since the foundation of the world.
For when I was hungry, you gave me [something] to eat; when I was thirsty, you gave me to drink; when I was a stranger, you welcomed me.
When I was naked, you gave clothes to me; when I was sick, you came to care for me; when I was in prison, you came to visit me.'
Then the righteous people will answer him respectfully, 'Lord, when did we see you being hungry and feed you, or being thirsty and give you [something] to drink?
When did we see you being a stranger and welcome you, or being naked and give clothes to you?
When did we see you being sick or being in prison, and we came to visit you?'
The Great King will answer them, 'I tell you the truth, whatever you did for one of my brothers who is the lowest, it is the same as having done it for me.'
Then he will speak also to those on the left side, 'You who are cursed, go from me into eternal fire that was prepared for the devil and its messengers.
For when I was hungry, you did not give me [anything] to eat; when I was thirsty, you did not give me to drink.
When I was a stranger, you did not welcome me; when I was naked, you did not give clothes to me; when I was sick and in prison, you did not come to care for me.'
Then they also will answer respectfully, 'Lord, when did we see you being hungry, or being thirsty, or being a stranger, or being naked, or being sick, or being in prison, and we did not serve you?'
Then he will answer them, 'I tell you the truth, whatever you did not do for one of the lowest of these, it is the same as not having done it for me.'
These people will go into eternal punishment, but the righteous will go into eternal life."
